description: People may 'koluma' around a shared effort; separate pieces may unite into one assembly. What had been separate now forms one group or whole. 'lorea' (connect) establishes a link, while 'shiwolu' (integrate) concerns parts working within a wider arrangement.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and release into middle-height back rounded 'o'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l', let air pass along its sides, and raise the back of the tongue while tightening the lip rounding for stressed high-back 'u'. Close the lips for voiced 'm', carry the sound through the nose, and open into central 'a'.
usage_notes: 'koluma' may be transitive when someone unites people or things, or intransitive with a plural subject. Noun position gives unity or the act of uniting. When people unite, purpose and consent remain facts for the surrounding account.

semantic_domains.activity: forming one group or whole from separate participants or parts
semantic_domains.community: people joining into a shared body or effort
semantic_domains.physical: separate pieces becoming one material assembly
